items.title: Community-hosted browser coding sandbox
items.oneliner: Free in-browser Python and HTML runner lets teachers and students share executable lessons without installs or logins.

items.title: Peer-to-peer networking stack is production ready
items.oneliner: Open networking stack gives apps stable device identities, NAT traversal, and direct QUIC connections without central servers.

items.title: Open coding model with downloadable weights
items.oneliner: Apache 2.0 coding model ships downloadable weights and low-latency inference for local agentic code generation.

items.title: Smart bulb serves banned books locally
items.oneliner: Hands-on build shows how to flash Tasmota and host a local ebook server from a Wi-Fi light bulb.

items.title: Homelab AI developer platform walkthrough
items.oneliner: Detailed homelab guide shows Git-integrated AI coding, container healthchecks, and guarded deployment workflows you can replicate.

items.title: Malicious npm backdoor teardown
items.oneliner: Security writeup dissects a fake hiring repo whose npm install executed remote commands, with concrete indicators to inspect.

items.title: E-bike firmware hack and repair
items.oneliner: Video walkthrough shows jailbreaking an e-bike controller, reverse engineering limits, and fixing hardware with custom printed parts.

items.title: NotebookLM business research workflow
items.oneliner: Step-by-step system turns NotebookLM into a decision memo pipeline with source gathering, structured briefs, and comparison tables.

items.title: Perlin noise generative art tutorial
items.oneliner: Practical Processing tutorial teaches particle systems driven by Perlin noise fields, with enough code to start generating visuals.

items.title: Unicorn CPU emulator toolkit
items.oneliner: Battle-tested CPU emulation engine lets you script x86, ARM, and MIPS execution for reverse engineering and fuzzing.
